result.markdown:
--- title: Linear Regression date: today date-format: long footer: "[IAFF 6501 Website](https://quant4ia.rocks/)" logo: images/iaff6501-logo.png format: revealjs: theme: [simple, custom.scss] transition: fade slide-number: true #multiplex: true chalkboard: true execute: echo: false message: false warning: false freeze: auto --- ::: {.cell} ::: ## Linear Model with Single Predictor
Goal: Estimate Democracy score ($\hat{Y_{i}}$) of a country given level of GDP per capita ($X_{i}$).
Or: Estimate relationship between GDP per capita and democracy. ## Linear Model with Single Predictor ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](week-10.1_files/figure-revealjs/model-data-1.png){width=960} ::: ::: # Estimate Model ##
Fit model using base R `lm()` with **formula syntax**
::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} mod_fit <- lm(lib_dem ~ log_wealth, data = modelData) ``` ::: ##
View results with `summary()`
$$\widehat{Democracy}_{i} = 0.13 + 0.12 * {loggdppc}_{i}$$ ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} summary(mod_fit) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Call: lm(formula = lib_dem ~ log_wealth, data = modelData) Residuals: Min 1Q Median 3Q Max -0.58804 -0.15009 0.03924 0.18502 0.40243 Coefficients: Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|) (Intercept) 0.008895 0.047694 0.186 0.852 log_wealth 0.129689 0.014524 8.929 6.35e-16 *** --- Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 Residual standard error: 0.2178 on 172 degrees of freedom (5 observations deleted due to missingness) Multiple R-squared: 0.3167, Adjusted R-squared: 0.3128 F-statistic: 79.73 on 1 and 172 DF, p-value: 6.353e-16 ``` ::: ::: ## Interpretation?
$$\widehat{Democracy}_{i} = 0.13 + 0.12 * {loggdppc}_{i}$$ ## Question
How do we get the "best" values for the slope and intercept? ## How would you draw the "best" line? ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](week-10.1_files/figure-revealjs/best-line-1.png){width=960} ::: ::: ## How would you draw the "best" line? ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](week-10.1_files/figure-revealjs/best-line2-1.png){width=960} ::: ::: ## Least squares regression
- Remember the residual is the difference between the actual value and the predicted value . . . - The regression line minimizes the sum of squared residuals. ## Least squares regression
- Residual for each point is: $e_i = y_i - \hat{y}_i$ - Least squares regression line minimizes $\sum_{i = 1}^n e_i^2$. . . . - Why do we square the residual? . . . - Why not take absolute value? - Principle: larger penalty for residuals further away - Math: makes the math easier and some nice properties (not our concern here...) ## Least squares regression ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](week-10.1_files/figure-revealjs/best-line3-1.png){width=960} ::: ::: ## Very Simple Example What should the slope and intercept be? ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](week-10.1_files/figure-revealjs/best-line4-1.png){width=960} ::: ::: ## Example $\hat{Y} = 0 + 1*X$ ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](week-10.1_files/figure-revealjs/best-line5-1.png){width=960} ::: ::: ## Example What is the sum of squared residuals? ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](week-10.1_files/figure-revealjs/best-line6-1.png){width=960} ::: ::: ## Example What is sum of squared residuals for $y = 0 + 0*X$? ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](week-10.1_files/figure-revealjs/best-line7-1.png){width=960} ::: ::: ## Example What is sum of squared residuals for $y = 0 + 0*X$? ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](week-10.1_files/figure-revealjs/best-line8-1.png){width=960} ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} (1-0)^2 + (2-0)^2 + (3-0)^2 ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] 14 ``` ::: ::: ## Example What is sum of squared residuals for $y = 0 + 2*X$? ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](week-10.1_files/figure-revealjs/best-line10-1.png){width=960} ::: ::: ## Example What is sum of squared residuals for $y = 0 + 2*X$? ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](week-10.1_files/figure-revealjs/best-line11-1.png){width=960} ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} (1-2)^2 + (2-4)^2 + (3-6)^2 ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] 14 ``` ::: ::: ## One more... What is sum of squared residuals for $y = 0 + -1*X$? ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](week-10.1_files/figure-revealjs/best-line13-1.png){width=960} ::: ::: ## One more... What is sum of squared residuals for $y = 0 + -1*X$? ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](week-10.1_files/figure-revealjs/best-line14-1.png){width=960} ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} (1+1)^2 + (2+2)^2 + (3+3)^2 ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] 56 ``` ::: ::: ## Cost Function Sum of Squared Residuals as function of possible values of $b$ ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](week-10.1_files/figure-revealjs/cost-function-1.png){width=960} ::: ::: ## Least Squares Regression
- When we estimate a least squares regression, it is looking for the line that minimizes sum of squared residuals - In the simple example, I set $a=0$ to make it easier. More complicated when searching for combination of $a$ and $b$ that minimize, but same basic idea ## Least Squares Regression
- There is a way to solve for this analytically for linear regression (i.e., by doing math...) -- They made us do this in grad school... . . . - In machine learning, people also use gradient descent algorithm in which the computer searches over possible combinations of $a$ and $b$ until it settles on the lowest point. ## Least Squares Regression ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](week-10.1_files/figure-revealjs/ssr-viz-1.png){width=960} ::: ::: ## Least Squares Regression ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](week-10.1_files/figure-revealjs/regression-line-1.png){width=960} ::: ::: ## Your Turn {.smaller}
Are democracies less corrupt?
- V-Dem includes a Political Corruption Index, which aggregates corruption in a number of spheres (see codebook for details). - The variable name is: *v2x_corr* : lower values mean less corruption - See started code [HERE](https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fo/8xpsrz27m0on1mc2pf5ya/h?rlkey=716y4lvy6uisq0gortq1as0sp&dl=0) ## Your Turn {.smaller}
**Are democracies less corrupt?**
::: {.smaller} - Filter the V-Dem data to only include the year 2019 - Make a scatterplot to visualize the relationship between democracy (X) and corruption (Y) (use the *v2x_libdem* variable for democracy) - Fit a linear model - Interpret results for the slope and intercept - For a country with the average (mean) level of democracy, what is the predicted level of corruption? ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html} ``` ::: ::: ## Create Your Own Model {.smaller}
- What is a theory that you would like to test with V-Dem data? - What is the dependent variable? - What is the independent variable? - Map out steps to wrangle the data and fit a regression model - What do you expect to find? - Now go ahead and wrangle the data - Fit the model - Interpret the coefficients and their significance - Did the results match your expectations?

include-after-body:
<script>
  // htmlwidgets need to know to resize themselves when slides are shown/hidden.
  // Fire the "slideenter" event (handled by htmlwidgets.js) when the current
  // slide changes (different for each slide format).
  (function () {
    // dispatch for htmlwidgets
    function fireSlideEnter() {
      const event = window.document.createEvent("Event");
      event.initEvent("slideenter", true, true);
      window.document.dispatchEvent(event);
    }

    function fireSlideChanged(previousSlide, currentSlide) {
      fireSlideEnter();

      // dispatch for shiny
      if (window.jQuery) {
        if (previousSlide) {
          window.jQuery(previousSlide).trigger("hidden");
        }
        if (currentSlide) {
          window.jQuery(currentSlide).trigger("shown");
        }
      }
    }

    // hookup for slidy
    if (window.w3c_slidy) {
      window.w3c_slidy.add_observer(function (slide_num) {
        // slide_num starts at position 1
        fireSlideChanged(null, w3c_slidy.slides[slide_num - 1]);
      });
    }

  })();
</script>
